Based on my search results, here's what I found about PatternFly's React 18 compatibility and version support in 2025:

## PatternFly React 18 Compatibility

### PatternFly 6 (Current)
PatternFly supports the 2 most recent versions of React at a time.(no content)PatternFly 6 currently supports React 17 and 18, with support for React 19 coming soon.

### React 19 Support (Q2 2025)
PatternFly now includes support for React 19 across all components and extensions, enabling you to benefit from new React features and performance enhancements.(no content)This update is non-breaking, and PatternFly 6 will continue to support React 17 and 18 in line with our support policy.(no content)For the Q2 2025 PatternFly release they made progress in a few of their key initiatives, including PatternFly 6 migration enablement, component animations, React 19 support, and the continued evolution of PatternFly AI.

### React Packages
PatternFly's React library contains a collection of components that you can use to build interfaces with consistent markup, styling, and behavior. It is composed of a number of React packages, which are published in the following npm packages: @patternfly/react-core: Most React core components.

Other packages include:
- @patternfly/react-charts: PatternFly charting components.
- @patternfly/react-code-editor: A PatternFly wrapper for the Monaco code editor.
- @patternfly/react-icons: PatternFly icons as React components.
- @patternfly/react-tokens: PatternFly's CSS variables, transformed into JavaScript objects for use with React.

### Historical Context
Previously, PatternFly offered experimental support for React 18. With the PatternFly 5 update, they performed the required dependency updates, rolling updates, and necessary testing to validate the support. PatternFly 5 became compatible with both React 17 and React 18.

In summary, as of 2025, PatternFly 6 fully supports React 17, 18, and now React 19, with React 19 support being added in the Q2 2025 release.

Based on my search, here's what I found about PatternFly React charts and topology visualization in 2025:

## PatternFly React Topology

PatternFly Topology is an open source utility that you can use to create a visual representation of all the applications within your project, their build status, and the components and services associated with them.(no content)Topology utilizes some of PatternFly's React components.

### Latest Releases (2025)

The latest version is v6.3.0, which added support for React 19 and replaced react-measure with resizeObserver.(no content)For the Q2 2025 PatternFly release, they completed their initiative to support React 19 across PatternFly.(no content)PatternFly now includes support for React 19 across all components and extensions. This update is non-breaking, and PatternFly 6 will continue to support React 17 and 18.

### Key Features

The topology library supports multiple layout options including Force, Dagre, Cola, etc. through registerLayoutFactory. You can use defaultLayoutFactory as a parameter if your application supports all layouts.(no content)registerComponentFactory lets you customize the components in your topology view (e.g. nodes, groups, and edges). You can use defaultComponentFactory as a parameter.

## PatternFly React Charts

PatternFly React charts live in their own package at @patternfly/react-charts. The examples are based on the Victory chart library, along with additional functionality, custom components, and theming for PatternFly.(no content)Victory is now an optional peer dependency, allowing future chart libraries to be installed without including Victory dependencies and vice versa.

### 2025 Package Versions

In January 2025, @patternfly/data-view@6.1.0 was published. The December 2024 release included @patternfly/react-charts@8.1.0 and @patternfly/react-topology@6.1.0.

### Upcoming in 2025

Planned updates include the full release of high contrast mode, updated website navigation, updating icons to align with Red Hat's brand standards, a new guided tour extension, and continued work toward WCAG 2.2 AA compliance.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about PatternFly v6 React components documentation for 2025:

## PatternFly v6 React Components Overview

PatternFly is Red Hat's open source design system. It consists of components, documentation, and code for building enterprise applications at scale.

### Key Q2 2025 Updates

For the Q2 2025 PatternFly release, they completed the initiative to support React 19 across PatternFly and made significant progress towards finalizing component animations.(no content)They also added new ChatBot features, new CLI guidelines, and updated docs for AI principles and motion guidelines.(no content)PatternFly now includes support for React 19 across all components and extensions, enabling users to benefit from new React features and performance enhancements. This update is non-breaking, and PatternFly 6 will continue to support React 17 and 18 in line with their support policy.

### React Package Structure

PatternFly's React library contains a collection of components that you can use to build interfaces with consistent markup, styling, and behavior. It is composed of several npm packages:
- `@patternfly/react-core`: Most React core components
- `@patternfly/react-charts`: PatternFly charting components
- `@patternfly/react-code-editor`: A PatternFly wrapper for the Monaco code editor
- `@patternfly/react-icons`: PatternFly icons as React components
- `@patternfly/react-tokens`: PatternFly's CSS variables, transformed into JavaScript objects for use with React

### Getting Started

Each PatternFly component already contains the CSS related to the component. In order to have consistent styles throughout the application, PatternFly Base CSS should be used in the project. This import statement should be placed before your main application component is imported: `import '@patternfly/react-core/dist/styles/base.css';`

### Migration Tools

For upgrading to PatternFly 6, you can run: `npx @patternfly/pf-codemods@latest <path to your source code> --v6`(no content)They've also published new documentation on using AI to streamline code migrations. This guide offers strategies for using AI to make it easier and faster to migrate your project to PatternFly React from other frameworks.

### Key Resources

- **Main Documentation**: https://www.patternfly.org/
- **GitHub Repository**: https://github.com/patternfly/patternfly-react
- **npm Package**: https://www.npmjs.com/package/@patternfly/react-core
- **Upgrade Guide**: https://www.patternfly.org/get-started/upgrade/

content.content:
# Page and PageSection Components Overview

## Layout Structure

The **Page** component defines basic page layouts with vertical or horizontal navigation. It uses a grid-based system that accommodates a masthead, optional sidebar, and main content area.

Key structural elements include:
- Masthead (top navigation bar)
- PageSidebar (optional left navigation)
- Main container (primary content area with rounded corners and borders)
- Multiple PageSection components for content organization

## Props Overview

### Page Component
The Page accepts numerous configuration options:
- `masthead`: Navigation component at the top
- `sidebar`: Optional vertical navigation (PageSidebar)
- `isContentFilled`: Enables sections to fill available vertical space
- `isManagedSidebar`: Automatically manages sidebar open/close state
- `breadcrumb`, `horizontalSubnav`: Optional navigation elements
- `mainComponent`: Defaults to 'main' HTML element
- `onPageResize`: Callback for responsive behavior

### PageSection Component
Configurable section properties include:
- `type`: Values like 'default', 'subnav', 'breadcrumb', 'tabs', 'wizard'
- `variant`: 'default' or 'secondary' background styling
- `isFilled`: Allows sections to expand vertically
- `padding`: Breakpoint-specific padding control
- `isWidthLimited` + `isCenterAligned`: Content centering options
- `hasShadowTop/Bottom`: Visual separation effects

## Sidebar Integration

The sidebar system provides flexible navigation:
- **PageSidebar** wraps sidebar content
- **PageSidebarBody** components render navigation or custom content
- `isManagedSidebar` prop eliminates manual state management
- `usePageInsets` aligns sidebar body padding with page margins
- `isFilled` property controls vertical space consumption

## Masthead Configuration

The masthead typically contains:
- "MastheadMain": Smaller area with toggle button and logo
- "MastheadContent": Main portion holding toolbars and menus
- "PageToggleButton": Sidebar toggle with optional hamburger animation

## Code Examples

The documentation provides multiple implementation patterns:

**Vertical Navigation**: Demonstrates "sidebar isSidebarOpen property helps facilitate opening and closing."

**Multiple Sidebar Bodies**: Shows stacking multiple PageSidebarBody components with different configurations for context selectors and navigation.

**Filled Sections**: Illustrates using `isContentFilled` on Page and `isFilled={true}` on child sections for space distribution.

**Width-Limited Content**: Shows `isWidthLimited` and `isCenterAligned` for constraining section width.

content.content:
# Card Component Overview

## Purpose
The Card component is a content container displaying entry-level information, commonly used in dashboards, galleries, and catalogs.

## Core Props

**Card Component:**
- `isClickable` / `isClicked` - Makes card actionable; `isClicked` shows current state
- `isSelectable` / `isSelected` - Enables selection with visual feedback
- `isExpanded` - Controls expandable card state
- `isCompact` / `isLarge` - Size modifiers (mutually exclusive)
- `isFullHeight` - Fills container height
- `isPlain` - Removes border and background
- `variant` - "default" or "secondary" background styling
- `isDisabled` - Disables interactive cards

**CardHeader:**
- `actions` - Add kebab menus, checkboxes, images
- `selectableActions` - Configure selection behavior
- `hasWrap` - Enables header wrapping
- `isToggleRightAligned` - Right-aligns expandable toggle

**CardHeaderSelectableActionsObject:**
- `variant` - "single" or "multiple" (for tile usage)
- `isHidden` - Hides radio/checkbox input
- `isChecked` - Selection state
- `onChange` - Selection change callback
- `onClickAction` - Action for clickable-only cards

## Structural Components

- `<CardTitle>` - Header text (supports HTML heading elements via `component` prop)
- `<CardBody>` - Content area; `isFilled={true}` by default to expand
- `<CardFooter>` - Footer content
- `<CardExpandableContent>` - Hidden content revealed on expansion

## Usage Patterns

**Basic Card:**
Combine CardHeader, CardTitle, CardBody, and CardFooter as needed.

**Selectable Cards:**
Pass `selectableActions` to CardHeader with `isSelected` state on Card for multi-select or single-select tiles.

**Actionable Cards:**
Use `selectableActions` with `onClickAction` or `to` prop for link navigation; supports `isExternalLink` flag.

**Actionable + Selectable:**
Combine both `isClickable` and `isSelectable` for cards with interactive content alongside selection.

**Expandable Cards:**
Set `isExpanded` prop with content in `<CardExpandableContent>`; caret toggles visibility.

## Customization

Multiple `<CardBody>` sections support visual content separation. Set `isFilled={false}` on non-primary bodies to prevent full-height expansion. CSS variables control spacing, colors, typography, and borders across themes.

content.content:
# PatternFly Button Component Overview

## Button Variants

The Button component supports multiple styling variants for different use cases:

**Primary**: "The most visually prominent variant. Use for the most important call to action."

**Secondary**: "Less visually prominent than primary buttons. Use for general actions on a page that require less emphasis."

**Tertiary**: "The least visually prominent variant. Use to retain a classic button format with less emphasis."

**Danger**: Can be applied independently or to other variants using the `isDanger` property for potentially destructive actions.

**Warning**: For actions that change important settings without being destructive or irreversible.

**Link**: "Labeled, but have no background or border. Use for actions that require less emphasis, actions that navigate users to another page within the same window, and/or actions that navigate to external pages in a new window."

**Plain**: "Have no styling and are intended to be labeled with icons."

**Control**: "Can be labeled with text or icons. Primarily intended to be paired with other controls in an input group."

**Stateful**: Display notification states with three options: `read`, `unread`, and `attention`.

## Key Props

- `variant`: Defines button style (primary, secondary, tertiary, danger, warning, link, plain, control, stateful)
- `isDisabled`: Disables the button using the disabled HTML attribute
- `isAriaDisabled`: Uses aria-disabled instead, allowing focus and tooltip support
- `size`: Options are 'default', 'sm' (small), or 'lg' (large/call-to-action)
- `isBlock`: Spans full width of parent element
- `isDanger`: Applies danger styling to secondary or link variants
- `isLoading`: Adds progress indicator styling
- `component`: Sets base component (defaults to button; can use "a" for links)
- `countOptions`: Adds badge count display via BadgeCountObject
- `isFavorite` / `isFavorited`: Creates animated favorite button
- `isHamburger`: Creates hamburger menu button with animations
- `isSettings`: Generates settings button with hover/focus animations
- `state`: For stateful variant ('read', 'unread', 'attention')

## Example Code Patterns

**Link as Button**:
```jsx
<Button variant="link" component="a" href="url">Link Text</Button>
```

**Router Integration**:
```jsx
<Button variant="link" component={(props) => <Link {...props} to="#" />}>
  Router link
</Button>
```

**With Icon**:
Buttons can include icons via the `icon` property with `iconPosition` set to 'start' or 'end'.

**Block Level**:
```jsx
<Button isBlock>Full width button</Button>
```

## Additional Features

- **Progress Indicators**: `isLoading` prop shows loading state
- **Disabled States**: Both `isDisabled` and `isAriaDisabled` available
- **Inline Links**: Use `isInline` property with link variant to wrap text naturally
- **Custom Components**: Callbacks can be passed to the `component` prop for fine-tuned customization

**Note**: No ButtonGroup component is documented on this page; the focus is on individual Button styling and functionality.

# PatternFly Button Component Overview

## Button Variants

The Button component supports multiple styling variants for different use cases:

**Primary**: "The most visually prominent variant. Use for the most important call to action."

**Secondary**: "Less visually prominent than primary buttons. Use for general actions on a page that require less emphasis."

**Tertiary**: "The least visually prominent variant. Use to retain a classic button format with less emphasis."

**Danger**: Can be applied independently or to other variants using the `isDanger` property for potentially destructive actions.

**Warning**: For actions that change important settings without being destructive or irreversible.

**Link**: "Labeled, but have no background or border. Use for actions that require less emphasis, actions that navigate users to another page within the same window, and/or actions that navigate to external pages in a new window."

**Plain**: "Have no styling and are intended to be labeled with icons."

**Control**: "Can be labeled with text or icons. Primarily intended to be paired with other controls in an input group."

**Stateful**: Display notification states with three options: `read`, `unread`, and `attention`.

## Key Props

- `variant`: Defines button style (primary, secondary, tertiary, danger, warning, link, plain, control, stateful)
- `isDisabled`: Disables the button using the disabled HTML attribute
- `isAriaDisabled`: Uses aria-disabled instead, allowing focus and tooltip support
- `size`: Options are 'default', 'sm' (small), or 'lg' (large/call-to-action)
- `isBlock`: Spans full width of parent element
- `isDanger`: Applies danger styling to secondary or link variants
- `isLoading`: Adds progress indicator styling
- `component`: Sets base component (defaults to button; can use "a" for links)
- `countOptions`: Adds badge count display via BadgeCountObject
- `isFavorite` / `isFavorited`: Creates animated favorite button
- `isHamburger`: Creates hamburger menu button with animations
- `isSettings`: Generates settings button with hover/focus animations
- `state`: For stateful variant ('read', 'unread', 'attention')

## Example Code Patterns

**Link as Button**:
```jsx
<Button variant="link" component="a" href="url">Link Text</Button>
```

**Router Integration**:
```jsx
<Button variant="link" component={(props) => <Link {...props} to="#" />}>
  Router link
</Button>
```

**With Icon**:
Buttons can include icons via the `icon` property with `iconPosition` set to 'start' or 'end'.

**Block Level**:
```jsx
<Button isBlock>Full width button</Button>
```

## Additional Features

- **Progress Indicators**: `isLoading` prop shows loading state
- **Disabled States**: Both `isDisabled` and `isAriaDisabled` available
- **Inline Links**: Use `isInline` property with link variant to wrap text naturally
- **Custom Components**: Callbacks can be passed to the `component` prop for fine-tuned customization

**Note**: No ButtonGroup component is documented on this page; the focus is on individual Button styling and functionality.

# PatternFly Charts Overview

## Available Chart Types

PatternFly supports the following chart types:

- **Area Chart**: "Use to show (potentially multiple) trends over a continuous scale"
- **Bar Chart**: For comparing categories and their values (vertical or horizontal)
- **Stacked Bar Chart**: For displaying categorical data across multiple variables
- **Bullet Chart**: "Use as an extension to the bar chart to measure data with thresholds"
- **Donut Chart**: "Use to show the relationship of a set of values to a whole"
- **Line Chart**: For comparing datasets or showing changes over time
- **Pie Chart**: For displaying percentages or proportional data
- **Sparkline**: "Use to show how values change over time for multiple sets of data"
- **Box Plot, Scatter, Sankey, and Threshold charts** are also available

## React-Charts Package

Charts are exclusively available in React. Developers should use the `@patternfly/react-charts` package from npm, which includes default light theme styling without additional imports.

## Theming & Dark Mode Support

To implement dark-themed charts:

1. Import the `@patternfly/patternfly` package
2. Add this stylesheet: `import '@patternfly/patternfly/patternfly-charts.css';`
3. Access chart variables for custom styling

## Victory Library Integration

PatternFly recommends Victory for "more advanced features" like brush and zoom functionality, with documentation available through their open-source guides.

## Key Features

- Interactive legends (hover to highlight, click to hide datasets)
- Brush and zoom magnification capabilities
- Responsive tooltips on chart element hover
- Accessibility-focused design practices

# PatternFly Masthead Component Overview

## Structure

The Masthead is a top navigation component containing global properties like branding, navigation, and settings. It requires two main sections:

**Core Components:**
- `<Masthead>` - Main container
- `<MastheadMain>` - Wraps `<PageToggleButton>` and `<MastheadBrand>`
- `<MastheadBrand>` - Contains `<MastheadLogo>`
- `<MastheadLogo>` - Logo wrapper
- `<MastheadContent>` - Contains page header toolbar and additional controls
- `<MastheadToggle>` - Optional sidebar toggle

## Key Props

**Masthead:**
- `display` - Layout type at breakpoints ('inline' or 'stack')
- `inset` - Padding values (insetNone to inset3xl)
- `children` - React nodes
- `className` - Additional CSS classes

**MastheadLogo:**
- `component` - Accepts ElementType or callback for custom link components
- Supports router integration via callback: `component={(props) => <Link {...props} to="#" />}`

## Integration Points

The Masthead integrates with `<Toolbar>` for header actions. The component has specialized CSS variables:
- `--pf-v6-c-masthead--c-toolbar--Width: 100%`
- `--pf-v6-c-masthead--c-toolbar--PaddingBlock: 0`

This allows seamless toolbar placement within the content section.

## Display Modes

- **Stack** (default on mobile): Logo above navigation, border separator
- **Inline** (default on md+): Logo and navigation side-by-side, compact layout

## Responsive Behavior

Breakpoint-based display switching supports sm, md, lg, xl, and 2xl sizes with independent layout control per breakpoint.

# PatternFly Label Component Overview

## Definition
A **label** is described as "a descriptive annotation that adds context to an element for clarity and convenience."

## Variants
The Label component supports two primary visual styles:
- **Filled**: Solid background with text
- **Outlined**: Transparent background with border
- **Compact**: Reduced padding variant
- **Overflow**: For displaying additional label counts
- **Add**: For creating new labels

## Color Options

### Non-Status Colors
Blue, teal, green, orange, purple, red, orange-red, grey, and yellow

### Status-Based Colors
- **Success**: Dark green background with white text
- **Warning**: Yellow background with dark text
- **Danger**: Dark red-orange with white text
- **Info**: Purple background with white text
- **Custom**: Teal background with white text

## Key Props

| Prop | Type | Description |
|------|------|-------------|
| `color` | string | Sets the label color (default: 'grey') |
| `variant` | string | Controls visual style (filled, outline, compact, overflow, add) |
| `isClickable` | boolean | Makes the label interactive |
| `isEditable` | boolean | Enables inline editing capability |
| `isDisabled` | boolean | Disables the label |
| `icon` | ReactNode | Adds an icon to the left of text |
| `href` | string | Converts label to an anchor element |
| `onClose` | function | Callback for close/remove action |

## Interactive Features

Labels support:
- **Clickable states** with hover effects
- **Editable mode**: Press Enter or Space to edit, Escape to cancel
- **Removable**: Close button with `onClose` callback
- **Router link support** via custom render prop
- **Text truncation** with tooltip on overflow

## Label Groups

The `LabelGroup` component manages multiple labels with features including:
- Categorical organization with `categoryName`
- Overflow handling (displays "+X more")
- Vertical or horizontal layout
- Editable labels with textarea support
- Add/remove functionality

## Accessibility
The component includes ARIA labels for close buttons and group semantics, supporting keyboard navigation and screen readers.

content.content:
# PatternFly 6 Upgrade Summary

## Key Breaking Changes

**Component Replacements:**
- "Chip" has been "Replaced with Label"
- "Tile" has been "Replaced with Card"
- Text component renamed to "Content"
- Expandable section removed `isActive` property

**Chart Library Restructuring:**
Victory-based charts moved to a dedicated directory. Import paths changed from `'@patternfly/react-charts'` to `'@patternfly/react-charts/victory'`. Victory is now an optional peer dependency.

## Migration Process

The upgrade requires four main steps:

1. **Run codemods first** - Execute `npx @patternfly/pf-codemods@latest <path> --v6 --fix` before manual changes to avoid compounding issues

2. **Remove CSS overrides** - "Any existing CSS overrides will be targeting outdated styles and will no longer work, so they must be updated or removed"

3. **Update variable names** - Use three specialized codemods:
   - class-name-updater (replaces pf-v5 with pf-v6 prefixes)
   - tokens-update (updates global CSS variables)
   - css-vars-updater (updates non-React file variables)

4. **Convert breakpoint units** - PatternFly 6 uses rem units instead of pixels; divide pixel values by 16

## Testing Considerations

Button and Select components have behavioral changes affecting test suites. Button's `aria-disabled` attribute behavior changed, and Select's menu structure was modified, requiring test query updates.

## Support Resources

The team offers assistance via Slack and GitHub Discussions for upgrade questions and styling concerns.

# PatternFly Grid Layout Components

## Overview
PatternFly's Grid is a layout system with two main components: **Grid** (container) and **GridItem** (child elements).

## Core Components

### Grid
The container component that accepts these key props:
- `hasGutter`: Adds spacing between children
- `span`: Column span (1-12) applied to all items
- Responsive props: `sm`, `md`, `lg`, `xl`, `xl2`
- `order`: Flex layout ordering

### GridItem
Individual grid cells with properties:
- `span`: Column span (1-12)
- `rowSpan`: Row span (1-12)
- `offset`/`[size]Offset`: Column offset values
- Device-specific variants: `md`, `lg`, `xl`, `xl2`
- Responsive row spans: `mdRowSpan`, `lgRowSpan`, etc.

## Responsive Breakpoints
The system supports five breakpoint sizes:
- `sm` (small device)
- `md` (medium device)
- `lg` (large device)
- `xl` (xLarge device)
- `xl2` (2xLarge device)

Each can be configured independently for different column spans.

## Key Features
- Maximum 12-column grid system
- Multi-row spanning capability
- Offset support for column positioning
- Gutter control with CSS variables
- Order-based element reordering

content.content:
# Toolbar Component Overview

## Structure

The Toolbar is a responsive container that organizes controls for managing datasets. It consists of several nested components:

- **Toolbar**: Root container with optional filtering and sticky positioning
- **ToolbarContent**: Row-based content container
- **ToolbarGroup**: Groups related items together
- **ToolbarItem**: Individual control elements
- **ToolbarToggleGroup**: Collapsible filter sections at breakpoints
- **ToolbarFilter**: Manages applied filters with label displays

## ToolbarItem Usage

Items can display various controls like buttons, filters, and pagination. The component accepts a `variant` property supporting "pagination," "label," "separator," and "expand-all" types. Items support responsive visibility and alignment properties across breakpoints (md, lg, xl, 2xl).

## ToolbarGroup Configuration

Groups organize related items and accept:
- `variant`: "filter-group," "action-group," "action-group-plain," or "label-group"
- Gap properties: `gap`, `columnGap`, `rowGap` with responsive breakpoint support
- Alignment options via `align` property

## Button Groups Implementation

Create button groups using ToolbarGroup with `variant="action-group"`. Multiple buttons nest within a single ToolbarGroup, with spacing controlled through gap properties. The documentation shows examples with primary, secondary, and tertiary button variants.

## Filter Toolbars

Use ToolbarToggleGroup with ToolbarFilter to create responsive filter interfaces. ToolbarFilter requires:
- `categoryName`: Unique identifier
- `labels`: Array of applied filter strings
- `deleteLabel`: Callback for removing individual filters
- `deleteLabelGroup`: Callback for clearing entire categories

Filters automatically collapse into overlay panels at specified breakpoints.

content.content:
# PatternFly Table Component Overview

## Basic Usage

PatternFly's Table component uses a composable, declarative approach. You structure tables by nesting `Thead`, `Tbody`, `Tr`, `Th`, and `Td` components within a `Table` wrapper. As the documentation states, "The `Table` component takes an explicit and declarative approach, and its implementation closely mirrors that of an HTML table."

Key setup requirements:
- Add `dataLabel` props to `Td` components for mobile context
- Place `<Caption>` as the first child if needed
- Set `variant="compact"` for condensed spacing

## Core Props

**Table component** accepts:
- `isExpandable` - enables expandable rows
- `isTreeTable` - creates tree table structure
- `isStickyHeader` - fixes header during scrolling
- `isStriped` - alternates row background colors
- `variant="compact"` - reduces spacing

**Selection handling** uses:
- `ThSelectType` on header cells
- `TdSelectType` on data cells with `OnSelect` event callback

## Sorting Implementation

Enable sorting by passing a `ThSortType` object to column headers with:
- `columnIndex` - identifies the sortable column
- `sortBy` - tracks current direction ('asc'/'desc')
- `onSort` - handles sort button clicks

The component provides "Wraps the content in a button and adds a sort icon" automatically.

## Actions & Expandability

**Actions column** uses `ActionsColumn` component with `IAction` items array. The `menuAppendTo` prop prevents menu clipping with sticky columns.

**Expandable rows** require:
- `TdExpandType` for expansion toggles
- `ExpandableRowContent` wrapper for child content
- `isExpanded` on both control and content rows

## Selection Patterns

Checkboxes and radio buttons use dedicated selection columns. The documentation notes shift+select functionality enables "intermediate rows' checkboxes" to be checked when holding shift.

# Navigation Component Overview

## Key Components

**NavList** serves as the container for navigation items, with configurable scroll behavior through `backScrollAriaLabel` and `forwardScrollAriaLabel` props.

**NavItem** renders individual navigation links with support for icons, flyouts, and custom click handlers. The component accepts a `to` prop for linking and `isActive` to mark current pages.

## Navigation Variants

The Navigation component supports three layout modes:
- **Default**: Vertical sidebar navigation
- **Horizontal**: "Organizes items across a horizontal row for top-level navigation"
- **Horizontal-subnav**: Combines horizontal layout with grouped sub-items

## Expandable Sections

NavExpandable allows collapsible navigation groups. The `onExpand` callback lets consumers manage expansion state externally, while `isExpanded` pragmatically controls collapse/expand behavior.

## Code Structure

Navigation items report selection through the `onSelect` callback with this signature:

```
(event, {groupId, itemId, to}) => void
```

The `onToggle` callback handles expansion state changes for grouped items.

## Visual Features

- Icons integrate via the `icon` prop on NavItem
- Active states highlight current location with `.pf-m-current` styling
- Flyout menus (Menu components) attach to items via the `flyout` prop
- "Press `space` or `right arrow` to open a flyout using the keyboard"

# Drawer Component Overview

## Core Behavior
A **drawer** is "a sliding panel that enters from outside of the viewport, which can be configured to either overlay content or create a sidebar by pushing content."

## Position Options
The drawer supports multiple positioning:
- **End** (default) - slides from right side
- **Start** - slides from left side
- **Bottom** - slides from bottom
- **Left/Right** (deprecated) - use start/end instead

## Key Props

**Drawer Component:**
- `isExpanded` (boolean) - controls visibility
- `isInline` (boolean) - displays panel and content side-by-side
- `isStatic` (boolean) - shows both elements permanently
- `position` - determines slide direction
- `onExpand` callback - fires after 250ms animation completes

**DrawerPanelContent:**
- `isResizable` - enables width/height adjustment
- `focusTrap` - manages keyboard focus when expanded
- `widths` - responsive width settings at different breakpoints
- `colorVariant` - background styling options

## Content Structure
Drawers contain:
- **DrawerSection** - optional header area
- **DrawerContent** - main content area
- **DrawerPanelContent** - sliding panel with header, description, body, and actions
- **DrawerHead/DrawerActions** - panel header components

## Interaction Features
- Focus trap support with automatic focus management
- Resizable panels with keyboard controls (5px increment default)
- Responsive breakpoint handling (md, lg, xl, 2xl)
- Smooth transitions with customizable timing

# Panel Component Overview

## Purpose
The Panel is a customizable container component designed to hold other components in flexible content layouts within PatternFly v6.4.0.

## Primary Uses
Based on the documentation, Panel serves as a general-purpose content container rather than specifically for side panels, drawers, or sliding content. It's structured with optional header, footer, and main body sections.

## Key Props

**Panel Component:**
- `children` - Content rendered inside the panel
- `isScrollable` - Flag to add scrollable styling
- `variant` - Style options: 'raised', 'bordered', or 'secondary'
- `className` - Custom CSS classes

**PanelMain Component:**
- `children` - Content for the panel main area
- `maxHeight` - Constrains height with unit values

**PanelMainBody, PanelHeader, PanelFooter:**
- Each accepts `children` and `className` props

## Available Variants

The documentation shows three visual variants:
- **Raised** - Includes box shadow for elevation
- **Bordered** - Adds 1px border styling
- **Secondary** - Uses secondary background color

## Layout Examples
The component supports configurations including:
- Basic panel with body only
- Header with divider + body
- Body + footer
- Complete header + body + footer structure
- Scrollable content with max-height constraints

The Panel uses CSS variables for theming and spacing customization throughout its structure.

# PatternFly v6 Research for MMS Migration > Research Date: 2026-01-05 > Sources: [PatternFly Official Documentation](https://www.patternfly.org/), [GitHub Repository](https://github.com/patternfly/patternfly-react) --- ## Package Overview PatternFly is Red Hat's open source design system for enterprise applications. Version 6.4.0 is current (October 2025). ### Core Packages | Package | Purpose | Version | |---------|---------|---------| | `@patternfly/react-core` | Primary component library | 6.4.0 | | `@patternfly/react-table` | Table components | 6.x | | `@patternfly/react-charts` | Victory-based charting | 8.1.0 | | `@patternfly/react-topology` | Graph/network visualization | 6.3.0 | | `@patternfly/react-icons` | Icon components | 6.x | | `@patternfly/react-tokens` | CSS design tokens | 6.x | | `@patternfly/react-styles` | CSS styling utilities | 6.x | ### Installation ```bash # Core installation bun add @patternfly/react-core @patternfly/react-icons # For charts (optional) bun add @patternfly/react-charts victory # For topology (separate repo) bun add @patternfly/react-topology # For tables (if needed beyond react-core) bun add @patternfly/react-table ``` ### Required CSS Import ```typescript // Place BEFORE main app component import import '@patternfly/react-core/dist/styles/base.css'; // For dark mode charts support import '@patternfly/patternfly/patternfly-charts.css'; ``` --- ## React Version Compatibility **PatternFly 6 supports React 17, 18, AND 19** (Q2 2025 update). This is ideal for MMS because: - Current stack uses React 18 (required by BlueprintJS 6) - Migration can happen without React version change - Future upgrade path to React 19 is available --- ## Core Components for MMS ### 1. Page Layout System The `Page` component provides the main application structure: ```tsx import { Page, PageSection, PageSidebar, PageSidebarBody, Masthead, MastheadMain, MastheadBrand, MastheadLogo, MastheadContent, MastheadToggle, PageToggleButton } from '@patternfly/react-core'; function App() { return ( } sidebar={} isManagedSidebar // Auto manages open/close state > {/* Main canvas goes here */} ); } ``` **Key Props:** - `isManagedSidebar` - Automatic sidebar state management - `isContentFilled` - Sections fill available vertical space - `breadcrumb`, `horizontalSubnav` - Optional navigation elements ### 2. Card Component Direct replacement for BlueprintJS Card: ```tsx import { Card, CardHeader, CardTitle, CardBody, CardFooter, CardExpandableContent } from '@patternfly/react-core'; // Basic card Train TRAIN-001 Maintenance scheduled // Selectable card (for task selection) Task content // Expandable card Details Expanded content ``` **Variants:** - `isCompact` - Smaller padding - `isLarge` - Larger sizing - `isPlain` - No border/background - `variant="secondary"` - Secondary background color ### 3. Label Component (Tag Replacement) Replaces BlueprintJS Tag: ```tsx import { Label, LabelGroup } from '@patternfly/react-core'; // Basic labels with colors // Status labels (for LOD indicator) // With icon // Removable label // Label group ``` **Colors Available:** - Non-status: blue, teal, green, orange, purple, red, orange-red, grey, yellow - Status: success (green), warning (yellow), danger (red), info (purple) **Variants:** - `filled` - Solid background (default) - `outline` - Border only - `compact` - Reduced padding ### 4. Button Component ```tsx import { Button } from '@patternfly/react-core'; // Variants // Sizes // With loading state // Disabled states // Allows tooltip ``` ### 5. Toolbar (for controls area) Replaces ButtonGroup and control containers: ```tsx import { Toolbar, ToolbarContent, ToolbarGroup, ToolbarItem } from '@patternfly/react-core'; {/* Action buttons group */} {/* Label item */} {/* Separator */} {/* Pagination (right aligned) */} ``` ### 6. Navigation (Sidebar) ```tsx import { Nav, NavList, NavItem, NavExpandable } from '@patternfly/react-core';
Dashboard Planning TRAIN-001 TRAIN-002
``` ### 7. Drawer (Side Panel for Details) ```tsx import { Drawer, DrawerContent, DrawerPanelContent, DrawerHead, DrawerActions, DrawerCloseButton, DrawerPanelBody } from '@patternfly/react-core'; Task Details {/* Task detail content */} }> {/* Main content (canvas) */} ``` **Position Options:** - `end` (default) - Right side - `start` - Left side - `bottom` - Bottom panel --- ## Layout System ### Grid Layout ```tsx import { Grid, GridItem } from '@patternfly/react-core'; Sidebar Main Content // Responsive Content ``` ### PageSection Types ```tsx Main content Sub navigation Breadcrumbs Tab navigation Wizard content ``` --- ## WebGL Integration Strategy PatternFly components are standard React components - no special handling needed for WebGL/Three.js: ### Approach 1: PageSection Container ```tsx import { Page, PageSection } from '@patternfly/react-core'; import { Canvas } from '@react-three/fiber'; function App() { return ( ); } ``` ### Approach 2: Card Container (for embedded canvas) ```tsx {/* Three.js content */} ``` ### Approach 3: Drawer with Canvas ```tsx }>
``` **Key Considerations:** 1. Use `padding={{ default: 'noPadding' }}` on PageSection for full-bleed canvas 2. Set explicit heights or use flexbox/grid for proper canvas sizing 3. Canvas needs `position: absolute/relative` within container 4. Use `isFilled` prop for sections that should expand --- ## BlueprintJS to PatternFly Migration Map | BlueprintJS | PatternFly | Notes | |-------------|------------|-------| | `Card` | `Card` | Nearly identical API | | `Tag` | `Label` | Same concept, different name | | `Button` | `Button` | Similar variants | | `ButtonGroup` | `ToolbarGroup` | Use with Toolbar | | `Navbar` | `Masthead` | Different structure | | `Intent.PRIMARY` | `variant="primary"` | Color as variant | | `Intent.DANGER` | `variant="danger"` or `isDanger` | | | `Intent.WARNING` | `variant="warning"` | | | `Intent.SUCCESS` | `color="green"` (Label) | | | `Collapse` | `CardExpandableContent` | Or `Expandable` | | `Drawer` | `Drawer` | Similar concept | | `Tabs` | `Tabs` | Available in react-core | | `Spinner` | `Spinner` | Available | | `ProgressBar` | `Progress` | Available | ### Intent Colors Mapping ```tsx // BlueprintJS Blue Red Orange Green // PatternFly equivalent // or status="danger" // or status="warning" // or status="success" ``` ### Button Migration ```tsx // BlueprintJS // PatternFly ``` --- ## Charts Integration PatternFly uses Victory library for charts: ```tsx import { Chart, ChartArea, ChartAxis, ChartLine } from '@patternfly/react-charts/victory'; // Basic line chart ``` **Note:** Victory is optional peer dependency in v6. Import from `@patternfly/react-charts/victory`. --- ## Topology Component For network/dependency visualization (future MMS feature): ```tsx import { Visualization, VisualizationProvider, VisualizationSurface, Model, GRAPH_LAYOUT_FORCE } from '@patternfly/react-topology'; const model: Model = { nodes: [...], edges: [...], graph: { id: 'graph', type: 'graph', layout: GRAPH_LAYOUT_FORCE } }; ``` --- ## Code Examples ### Complete App Shell ```tsx import React, { useState } from 'react'; import '@patternfly/react-core/dist/styles/base.css'; import { Page, PageSection, PageSidebar, PageSidebarBody, Masthead, MastheadMain, MastheadBrand, MastheadLogo, MastheadContent, MastheadToggle, PageToggleButton, Toolbar, ToolbarContent, ToolbarGroup, ToolbarItem, Button, Label, Nav, NavList, NavItem, Drawer, DrawerContent, DrawerPanelContent, DrawerHead, DrawerActions, DrawerCloseButton, DrawerPanelBody } from '@patternfly/react-core'; import { BarsIcon } from '@patternfly/react-icons'; import { Canvas } from '@react-three/fiber'; function App() { const [sidebarOpen, setSidebarOpen] = useState(true); const [detailsOpen, setDetailsOpen] = useState(false); const [lod, setLod] = useState<'MACRO' | 'MESO' | 'MICRO'>('MESO'); const masthead = ( setSidebarOpen(!sidebarOpen)} > MMS Planning ); const sidebar = (
Planning Reports Settings
); const detailsPanel = ( Task Details setDetailsOpen(false)} /> {/* Task details content */} ); return (
{/* R3F content */}
); } export default App; ``` ### Train Sidebar Component ```tsx import { Card, CardBody, Label } from '@patternfly/react-core'; interface TrainSidebarProps { trains: Train[]; visibleRange: [number, number]; } export function TrainSidebar({ trains, visibleRange }: TrainSidebarProps) { const [start, end] = visibleRange; const visibleTrains = trains.slice(start, end); return (
{visibleTrains.map((train, i) => ( {train.id} ))}
); } ``` --- ## Gotchas and Considerations ### 1. CSS Import Order Matters ```tsx // CORRECT: PatternFly CSS before app styles import '@patternfly/react-core/dist/styles/base.css'; import './App.css'; // Your custom styles after ``` ### 2. Chip Component Removed PatternFly 6 replaced `Chip` with `Label`. If migrating from PatternFly 5: ```tsx // Old (v5) Filter // New (v6) ``` ### 3. Text Component Renamed ```tsx // Old import { Text, TextContent } from '@patternfly/react-core'; // New (v6) import { Content } from '@patternfly/react-core'; ``` ### 4. CSS Variable Prefix Changed ```css /* PatternFly 5 */ --pf-v5-global--primary-color--100 /* PatternFly 6 */ --pf-v6-global--primary-color--100 ``` Use codemods for automatic updates: ```bash npx @patternfly/pf-codemods@latest ./src --v6 --fix ``` ### 5. Victory is Optional Peer Dependency If using charts, install Victory explicitly: ```bash bun add victory ``` Import from victory subdirectory: ```tsx import { Chart } from '@patternfly/react-charts/victory'; ``` ### 6. Button aria-disabled Behavior Changed Tests may need updating if checking disabled state: ```tsx // Test updates needed expect(button).toHaveAttribute('aria-disabled', 'true'); // instead of expect(button).toBeDisabled(); ``` ### 7. Rem Units for Breakpoints PatternFly 6 uses rem instead of pixels for breakpoints: ```css /* Convert: divide by 16 */ /* 768px â†’ 48rem */ /* 992px â†’ 62rem */ /* 1200px â†’ 75rem */ ``` ### 8. No ButtonGroup Component Use `ToolbarGroup` instead: ```tsx // BlueprintJS // PatternFly ``` ### 9. Full Height Canvas Setup ```tsx {/* Content */} ``` --- ## Migration Checklist - [ ] Install PatternFly packages - [ ] Add base CSS import (before app CSS) - [ ] Replace Card imports - [ ] Replace Tag with Label - [ ] Replace ButtonGroup with ToolbarGroup - [ ] Update Navbar to Masthead structure - [ ] Replace Intent.* with variant/color props - [ ] Run PatternFly codemods for automatic fixes - [ ] Test all interactive components - [ ] Verify canvas sizing in PatternFly layout - [ ] Update any CSS variable references (v5 â†’ v6) --- ## Resources - [PatternFly Documentation](https://www.patternfly.org/) - [GitHub Repository](https://github.com/patternfly/patternfly-react) - [Upgrade Guide](https://www.patternfly.org/get-started/upgrade/) - [Release Highlights](https://www.patternfly.org/get-started/release-highlights/) - [React Training](https://www.patternfly.org/training/react-fundamentals-training/) - [Topology Documentation](https://www.patternfly.org/topology/about-topology/) - [Charts Documentation](https://www.patternfly.org/charts/about-charts/)

Version 6.4.0 is current (October 2025). ### Core Packages | Package | Purpose | Version | |---------|---------|---------| | `@patternfly/react-core` | Primary component library | 6.4.0 | | `@patternfly/react-table` | Table components | 6.x | | `@patternfly/react-charts` | Victory-based charting | 8.1.0 | | `@patternfly/react-topology` | Graph/network visualization | 6.3.0 | | `@patternfly/react-icons` | Icon components | 6.x | | `@patternfly/react-tokens` | CSS design tokens | 6.x | | `@patternfly/react-styles` | CSS styling utilities | 6.x | ### Installation ```bash # Core installation bun add @patternfly/react-core @patternfly/react-icons # For charts (optional) bun add @patternfly/react-charts victory # For topology (separate repo) bun add @patternfly/react-topology # For tables (if needed beyond react-core) bun add @patternfly/react-table ``` ### Required CSS Import ```typescript // Place BEFORE main app component import import '@patternfly/react-core/dist/styles/base.css'; // For dark mode charts support import '@patternfly/patternfly/patternfly-charts.css'; ``` --- ## React Version Compatibility **PatternFly 6 supports React 17, 18, AND 19** (Q2 2025 update). This is ideal for MMS because: - Current stack uses React 18 (required by BlueprintJS 6) - Migration can happen without React version change - Future upgrade path to React 19 is available --- ## Core Components for MMS ### 1. Page Layout System The `Page` component provides the main application structure: ```tsx import { Page, PageSection, PageSidebar, PageSidebarBody, Masthead, MastheadMain, MastheadBrand, MastheadLogo, MastheadContent, MastheadToggle, PageToggleButton } from '@patternfly/react-core'; function App() { return ( } sidebar={} isManagedSidebar // Auto manages open/close state > {/* Main canvas goes here */} ); } ``` **Key Props:** - `isManagedSidebar` - Automatic sidebar state management - `isContentFilled` - Sections fill available vertical space - `breadcrumb`, `horizontalSubnav` - Optional navigation elements ### 2. Card Component Direct replacement for BlueprintJS Card: ```tsx import { Card, CardHeader, CardTitle, CardBody, CardFooter, CardExpandableContent } from '@patternfly/react-core'; // Basic card Train TRAIN-001 Maintenance scheduled // Selectable card (for task selection) Task content // Expandable card Details Expanded content ``` **Variants:** - `isCompact` - Smaller padding - `isLarge` - Larger sizing - `isPlain` - No border/background - `variant="secondary"` - Secondary background color ### 3. Label Component (Tag Replacement) Replaces BlueprintJS Tag: ```tsx import { Label, LabelGroup } from '@patternfly/react-core'; // Basic labels with colors // Status labels (for LOD indicator) // With icon // Removable label // Label group ``` **Colors Available:** - Non-status: blue, teal, green, orange, purple, red, orange-red, grey, yellow - Status: success (green), warning (yellow), danger (red), info (purple) **Variants:** - `filled` - Solid background (default) - `outline` - Border only - `compact` - Reduced padding ### 4. Button Component ```tsx import { Button } from '@patternfly/react-core'; // Variants // Sizes // With loading state // Disabled states // Allows tooltip ``` ### 5. Toolbar (for controls area) Replaces ButtonGroup and control containers: ```tsx import { Toolbar, ToolbarContent, ToolbarGroup, ToolbarItem } from '@patternfly/react-core'; {/* Action buttons group */} {/* Label item */} {/* Separator */} {/* Pagination (right aligned) */} ``` ### 6. Navigation (Sidebar) ```tsx import { Nav, NavList, NavItem, NavExpandable } from '@patternfly/react-core';
Dashboard Planning TRAIN-001 TRAIN-002
``` ### 7. Drawer (Side Panel for Details) ```tsx import { Drawer, DrawerContent, DrawerPanelContent, DrawerHead, DrawerActions, DrawerCloseButton, DrawerPanelBody } from '@patternfly/react-core'; Task Details {/* Task detail content */} }> {/* Main content (canvas) */} ``` **Position Options:** - `end` (default) - Right side - `start` - Left side - `bottom` - Bottom panel --- ## Layout System ### Grid Layout ```tsx import { Grid, GridItem } from '@patternfly/react-core'; Sidebar Main Content // Responsive Content ``` ### PageSection Types ```tsx Main content Sub navigation Breadcrumbs Tab navigation Wizard content ``` --- ## WebGL Integration Strategy PatternFly components are standard React components - no special handling needed for WebGL/Three.js: ### Approach 1: PageSection Container ```tsx import { Page, PageSection } from '@patternfly/react-core'; import { Canvas } from '@react-three/fiber'; function App() { return ( ); } ``` ### Approach 2: Card Container (for embedded canvas) ```tsx {/* Three.js content */} ``` ### Approach 3: Drawer with Canvas ```tsx }>
``` **Key Considerations:** 1. Use `padding={{ default: 'noPadding' }}` on PageSection for full-bleed canvas 2. Set explicit heights or use flexbox/grid for proper canvas sizing 3. Canvas needs `position: absolute/relative` within container 4. Use `isFilled` prop for sections that should expand --- ## BlueprintJS to PatternFly Migration Map | BlueprintJS | PatternFly | Notes | |-------------|------------|-------| | `Card` | `Card` | Nearly identical API | | `Tag` | `Label` | Same concept, different name | | `Button` | `Button` | Similar variants | | `ButtonGroup` | `ToolbarGroup` | Use with Toolbar | | `Navbar` | `Masthead` | Different structure | | `Intent.PRIMARY` | `variant="primary"` | Color as variant | | `Intent.DANGER` | `variant="danger"` or `isDanger` | | | `Intent.WARNING` | `variant="warning"` | | | `Intent.SUCCESS` | `color="green"` (Label) | | | `Collapse` | `CardExpandableContent` | Or `Expandable` | | `Drawer` | `Drawer` | Similar concept | | `Tabs` | `Tabs` | Available in react-core | | `Spinner` | `Spinner` | Available | | `ProgressBar` | `Progress` | Available | ### Intent Colors Mapping ```tsx // BlueprintJS Blue Red Orange Green // PatternFly equivalent // or status="danger" // or status="warning" // or status="success" ``` ### Button Migration ```tsx // BlueprintJS // PatternFly ``` --- ## Charts Integration PatternFly uses Victory library for charts: ```tsx import { Chart, ChartArea, ChartAxis, ChartLine } from '@patternfly/react-charts/victory'; // Basic line chart ``` **Note:** Victory is optional peer dependency in v6. Import from `@patternfly/react-charts/victory`. --- ## Topology Component For network/dependency visualization (future MMS feature): ```tsx import { Visualization, VisualizationProvider, VisualizationSurface, Model, GRAPH_LAYOUT_FORCE } from '@patternfly/react-topology'; const model: Model = { nodes: [...], edges: [...], graph: { id: 'graph', type: 'graph', layout: GRAPH_LAYOUT_FORCE } }; ``` --- ## Code Examples ### Complete App Shell ```tsx import React, { useState } from 'react'; import '@patternfly/react-core/dist/styles/base.css'; import { Page, PageSection, PageSidebar, PageSidebarBody, Masthead, MastheadMain, MastheadBrand, MastheadLogo, MastheadContent, MastheadToggle, PageToggleButton, Toolbar, ToolbarContent, ToolbarGroup, ToolbarItem, Button, Label, Nav, NavList, NavItem, Drawer, DrawerContent, DrawerPanelContent, DrawerHead, DrawerActions, DrawerCloseButton, DrawerPanelBody } from '@patternfly/react-core'; import { BarsIcon } from '@patternfly/react-icons'; import { Canvas } from '@react-three/fiber'; function App() { const [sidebarOpen, setSidebarOpen] = useState(true); const [detailsOpen, setDetailsOpen] = useState(false); const [lod, setLod] = useState<'MACRO' | 'MESO' | 'MICRO'>('MESO'); const masthead = ( setSidebarOpen(!sidebarOpen)} > MMS Planning ); const sidebar = (
Planning Reports Settings
); const detailsPanel = ( Task Details setDetailsOpen(false)} /> {/* Task details content */} ); return (
{/* R3F content */}
); } export default App; ``` ### Train Sidebar Component ```tsx import { Card, CardBody, Label } from '@patternfly/react-core'; interface TrainSidebarProps { trains: Train[]; visibleRange: [number, number]; } export function TrainSidebar({ trains, visibleRange }: TrainSidebarProps) { const [start, end] = visibleRange; const visibleTrains = trains.slice(start, end); return (
{visibleTrains.map((train, i) => ( {train.id} ))}
); } ``` --- ## Gotchas and Considerations ### 1. CSS Import Order Matters ```tsx // CORRECT: PatternFly CSS before app styles import '@patternfly/react-core/dist/styles/base.css'; import './App.css'; // Your custom styles after ``` ### 2. Chip Component Removed PatternFly 6 replaced `Chip` with `Label`. If migrating from PatternFly 5: ```tsx // Old (v5) Filter // New (v6) ``` ### 3. Text Component Renamed ```tsx // Old import { Text, TextContent } from '@patternfly/react-core'; // New (v6) import { Content } from '@patternfly/react-core'; ``` ### 4. CSS Variable Prefix Changed ```css /* PatternFly 5 */ --pf-v5-global--primary-color--100 /* PatternFly 6 */ --pf-v6-global--primary-color--100 ``` Use codemods for automatic updates: ```bash npx @patternfly/pf-codemods@latest ./src --v6 --fix ``` ### 5. Victory is Optional Peer Dependency If using charts, install Victory explicitly: ```bash bun add victory ``` Import from victory subdirectory: ```tsx import { Chart } from '@patternfly/react-charts/victory'; ``` ### 6. Button aria-disabled Behavior Changed Tests may need updating if checking disabled state: ```tsx // Test updates needed expect(button).toHaveAttribute('aria-disabled', 'true'); // instead of expect(button).toBeDisabled(); ``` ### 7. Rem Units for Breakpoints PatternFly 6 uses rem instead of pixels for breakpoints: ```css /* Convert: divide by 16 */ /* 768px â†’ 48rem */ /* 992px â†’ 62rem */ /* 1200px â†’ 75rem */ ``` ### 8. No ButtonGroup Component Use `ToolbarGroup` instead: ```tsx // BlueprintJS // PatternFly ``` ### 9. Full Height Canvas Setup ```tsx {/* Content */} ``` --- ## Migration Checklist - [ ] Install PatternFly packages - [ ] Add base CSS import (before app CSS) - [ ] Replace Card imports - [ ] Replace Tag with Label - [ ] Replace ButtonGroup with ToolbarGroup - [ ] Update Navbar to Masthead structure - [ ] Replace Intent.* with variant/color props - [ ] Run PatternFly codemods for automatic fixes - [ ] Test all interactive components - [ ] Verify canvas sizing in PatternFly layout - [ ] Update any CSS variable references (v5 â†’ v6) --- ## Resources - [PatternFly Documentation](https://www.patternfly.org/) - [GitHub Repository](https://github.com/patternfly/patternfly-react) - [Upgrade Guide](https://www.patternfly.org/get-started/upgrade/) - [Release Highlights](https://www.patternfly.org/get-started/release-highlights/) - [React Training](https://www.patternfly.org/training/react-fundamentals-training/) - [Topology Documentation](https://www.patternfly.org/topology/about-topology/) - [Charts Documentation](https://www.patternfly.org/charts/about-charts/)

## Pesquisa PatternFly v6 Concluida

Criei o arquivo de pesquisa completo em `/Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/mms/system/_migration_memory/10_patternfly.md`.

### Resumo dos Principais Achados

**Compatibilidade React:**
- PatternFly 6 suporta React 17, 18 e 19 (atualizado Q2 2025)
- Ideal para MMS pois mantemos React 18 sem mudancas

**Pacotes Principais:**
| Pacote | Versao | Proposito |
|--------|--------|-----------|
| `@patternfly/react-core` | 6.4.0 | Componentes principais |
| `@patternfly/react-table` | 6.x | Tabelas |
| `@patternfly/react-charts` | 8.1.0 | Graficos (Victory) |
| `@patternfly/react-topology` | 6.3.0 | Visualizacao de grafos |

**Mapeamento BlueprintJS -> PatternFly:**
| BlueprintJS | PatternFly | Notas |
|-------------|------------|-------|
| Card | Card | API quase identica |
| Tag | Label | Mesmo conceito |
| Button | Button | Variants similares |
| ButtonGroup | ToolbarGroup | Dentro de Toolbar |
| Navbar | Masthead | Estrutura diferente |
| Intent.PRIMARY | `variant="primary"` | Cor como prop |
| Intent.DANGER | `variant="danger"` | |

**Integracao WebGL:**
- PatternFly e React padrao - sem restricoes para Three.js/R3F
- `PageSection` com `isFilled` e `padding={{ default: 'noPadding' }}` para canvas fullscreen
- `Drawer` para paineis de detalhes laterais

**Alertas de Migracao:**
1. Chip removido (usar Label)
2. Text renomeado para Content
3. Prefixos CSS mudaram (pf-v5 -> pf-v6)
4. Victory e peer dependency opcional
5. Nao existe ButtonGroup - usar ToolbarGroup

O documento inclui exemplos de codigo completos, incluindo um App Shell completo com Masthead, Sidebar, Drawer e integracao com Canvas R3F.
